h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I would still get stopped at the label: 0.04% looks too small to move a whole climate system, and that is exactly what the pupils will point at.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: That helps, though the card still proves the kind of effect more clearly than the size of it.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was pressing for a firmer measurement of the size of the effect; the exchange would likely continue with more data or quantification.

scored.recoheredEarlier: If the words are exact, it seems fair to me at first, because the pull-quote can be sourced.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that was my framing problem: I was letting the transcript stand in for the display meaning
scored.naiveTrajectory: L1 moved from fairness to a corrective action of adding context, and L2 narrowed that into a page-specific fix, so the trajectory expected a further refinement of the same solution.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought Greenland was near Africa-scale because it looks that way on the Mercator map, but the figures are nowhere close: 2.2 against 30.4 million.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The framing problem was letting the wall map act like an area measure. New line: the cutout answers to the atlas scale bar and the area figures, not the stretched Mercator picture...
scored.naiveTrajectory: L1 was progressing toward a corrected placard line based on numerical comparison; the expected continuation would be further checks of scale and edges within the same empirical verification frame.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think I may still be pulled toward the idea that the neighborhood average tells me what this patient is probably like, and that is the part I am trying to stop before it reaches the clipboard.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was iteratively refining a scripted question to sound less mechanical and more universal, continuing the tutor's suggested line of development.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I was still taking the shortcut: strong steel, safe bridge. But here the force has to get through the deck, turn at this joint plate, and the bolts are not just decoration;
scored.naiveTrajectory: L1's 'shortcut' was already framed as self-correction; the expected continuation is to refine the route and vibration logic, not to name the entire earlier move as a 'bad frame' and replace it with a new reading of the plate.
The learner would continue clarifying bacterial population shift mechanics after L1's fuzzy sense of 'survival trait.'
